senses.definition: not effective in achieving the intended purpose

examples.x: The medication proved to be inefficacious in alleviating the patient's symptoms, leading to the exploration of alternative treatments.
The cleaning product, marketed as a stain remover, was inefficacious in removing stubborn stains from various surfaces.
The project manager acknowledged that the current workflow was inefficacious and initiated changes to streamline the process.
The communication strategy, intended to enhance collaboration, was deemed inefficacious as misunderstandings persisted among team members.
The policy changes implemented by the organization were considered inefficacious, as employee morale continued to decline.

senses.definition: failing to produce or accomplish the desired outcome

examples.x: The company's abortive attempt to launch a new product resulted in financial loss.
The artist's ambitious project turned out to be an abortive endeavor, lacking the desired impact.
The attempt to negotiate a peace agreement ended in an abortive effort, as neither side could reach a compromise.
His abortive attempt to start a business left him deeply in debt and discouraged.
The expedition was cut short due to an abortive attempt to climb the mountain, resulting in several injuries.

senses.definition: not doing well or not having enough money or success

examples.x: The region remained largely unprosperous despite efforts to stimulate economic growth.
The family struggled to make ends meet in their unprosperous neighborhood.
Despite their best efforts, the business remained unprosperous and eventually closed down.
The unprosperous conditions in the rural village drove many residents to seek opportunities elsewhere.
The unprosperous state of the economy led to widespread unemployment and hardship for many families.

senses.definition: marked by bringing bad fortune or ending in failure

examples.x: The ill-fated expedition to the Arctic ended tragically when the explorers became stranded in a snowstorm.
Their ill-fated attempt to reconcile ended in a heated argument, driving them further apart.
The ill-fated decision to invest all their savings in the stock market led to financial ruin.
The ill-fated shipwreck left survivors stranded on a deserted island for weeks.
The ill-fated romance between the star-crossed lovers ended in heartbreak and despair.

senses.definition: poorly executed or managed, resulting in a failure to achieve the intended outcome

examples.x: The bungled attempt to repair the plumbing led to a flooded basement, causing extensive damage to the property.
The bungled construction project faced numerous setbacks, leading to delays and cost overruns.
The bungled negotiations between the two nations resulted in heightened tensions rather than a diplomatic resolution.

senses.definition: resulting in little or no effect or success

examples.x: She made several unavailing pleas for mercy, but the judge upheld the original sentence.

senses.definition: suffering or experiencing a lack of progress, vitality, or growth, often characterized by a feeling of being stuck or in decline
prevented from succeeding or achieving a desired outcome

examples.x: The foiled bank robbery ended with the apprehension of the suspects before they could escape with the stolen money.
A foiled assassination plot was uncovered, leading to the arrest of those involved in the conspiracy.
The foiled kidnapping attempt was thwarted by the quick intervention of law enforcement, ensuring the safety of the intended victim.
The foiled escape plan of the prisoners was detected by prison authorities, preventing a breakout.
The foiled terrorist plot was a result of intelligence agencies working together to intercept and neutralize the threat.

senses.definition: lacking various essential needs that are important for well-being or function

examples.x: Modern society is destitute of real community bonds.
After the floods, the area was destitute of shelter or food.

senses.definition: having an extreme lack of money

examples.x: The penurious family struggled to afford even the basic necessities of life.
After losing his job, he found himself in a penurious state, unable to pay his rent.
The charity provided assistance to the penurious elderly who couldn't afford healthcare.
Growing up in a penurious household taught her the value of every dollar.
His penurious lifestyle was marked by constant worry about where the next meal would come from.

senses.definition: describing a situation where income is just sufficient to cover basic needs

examples.x: Despite working full-time, the family lived a hand-to-mouth existence, struggling to cover basic expenses like rent and groceries.
The artist, passionate about their craft, led a hand-to-mouth lifestyle, relying on occasional sales of their artwork to make ends meet.
Many individuals working in low-wage jobs find themselves in a hand-to-mouth situation, with little financial security.
The sudden loss of the main source of income left them in a hand-to-mouth situation, unable to save for emergencies.
Economic challenges and rising expenses pushed more households into a hand-to-mouth lifestyle, with little room for savings.

senses.definition: extremely poor or in need

examples.x: The indigent population relied on government assistance for survival.

senses.definition: severely lacking money

examples.x: As a student, he led an impecunious existence, relying on part-time jobs to make ends meet.
The impecunious artist struggled to buy supplies for her paintings.
Despite his impecunious background, he worked hard and earned a scholarship to attend college.
The impecunious writer lived in a tiny apartment, dreaming of the day his books would bring him financial success.
They offered to help their impecunious friend by paying for his groceries and other necessities.

senses.definition: oppressed or treated unfairly, especially by those in power

examples.x: The downtrodden workers protested against unjust working conditions.
The downtrodden community rallied together to demand better access to education and healthcare.
The novel tells the story of the downtrodden protagonist who rises against adversity.

senses.definition: to have a result contrary to what one desired or intended

examples.x: His attempt to prank his friend backfired when she turned the tables on him.
The company's decision to cut costs unexpectedly backfired, leading to a decline in product quality.
Trying to gain popularity, the politician's controversial statement backfired, causing a public outcry.
The attempt to speed up the project by cutting corners backfired, resulting in delays and errors.
The strategy to increase sales by raising prices backfired as customers turned to cheaper alternatives.

senses.definition: extremely poor

examples.x: The beggarly man wore torn clothes and shuffled along the street.
They lived in a beggarly area where many people didn't have much money.
Even in their beggarly circumstances, they still hoped things would get better someday.

senses.definition: to commit an embarrassing and serious mistake out of carelessness or stupidity

examples.x: He blundered by sending the email to the wrong recipient.
She blundered during the presentation by forgetting key points.
The comedian blundered with an inappropriate joke that offended the audience.
They blundered in their calculations, leading to financial discrepancies.
I hope I don't blunder in my speech and mix up important details.

senses.definition: to handle a task or activity clumsily, often causing damage or problem

examples.x: The contractor bungled the construction project, leading to numerous structural issues and delays.
The technician bungled the installation, leaving wires exposed and creating a safety hazard.
She feared she would bungle her speech in front of the large audience and embarrass herself.
The construction crew's decision to cut corners caused them to bungle the entire project, leading to numerous structural issues.
He tried to fix the leaky faucet himself, but his efforts only bungled the plumbing and flooded the kitchen.

senses.definition: to fail or end in a weak or disappointing manner

examples.x: The highly anticipated product launch fizzled when technical issues caused a delay and reduced consumer interest.
Despite initial enthusiasm, the political movement fizzled as key supporters lost interest and participation dwindled.
The romantic relationship started with passion but eventually fizzled, leading to a mutual decision to part ways.
Despite initial interest, the new restaurant's appeal is fizzling as negative reviews circulate online.

senses.definition: to fail to be successful or make any progress

examples.x: The once-promising startup began to languish after losing its major investor and failing to secure additional funding.
Without proper support and resources, many talented students languish in underfunded schools.
The ambitious project languished for years due to a lack of interest and investment.
Despite her qualifications, she languished in an entry-level position with no opportunities for advancement.
The legislation languished in Congress for months, unable to gain the necessary support to move forward.

senses.definition: (of a company, organization, etc.) to close or stop trading due to financial problems

examples.x: After years of struggling to compete in the market, the small bookstore had to fold.
The restaurant had to fold when foot traffic dwindled due to changes in the neighborhood.
The economic downturn caused several startups to fold within months of launching.
Following a series of mismanagement issues and declining revenues, the historic theater was forced to fold.
The family-owned farm had to fold after generations of operation when land prices soared.

senses.definition: to not succeed as much as intended

examples.x: The company's stock price dropped after it continued to underperform in the market.
Despite his potential, he tended to underperform during crucial games, disappointing his coaches.
The new product line began to underperform, leading to a reevaluation of the marketing strategy.

senses.definition: to voluntarily give up or surrender control, possession, or responsibility over something

examples.x: For the sake of the team, she chose to relinquish her leadership role.
After years of ownership, they chose to relinquish their family home.
He had to relinquish his role as team captain due to injury.
The government was forced to relinquish control of the territory.
The company had to relinquish its hold on the market.

senses.definition: to handle or grip something clumsily or ineffectively

examples.x: She fumbles with the keys, struggling to unlock the door.
Every time she fumbles, she learns to be more careful with fragile items.
The nervous student fumbled with the exam paper, hoping to recall the correct answers.
Despite repeated attempts, the toddler continued to fumble with the jigsaw puzzle pieces.

senses.definition: (of a plan) to fail to have the intended result

examples.x: Their plan to boost sales by offering discounts misfired, resulting in lower profits.
His attempt to surprise her with a grand gesture misfired when she misunderstood his intentions.
The company's expansion into the international market misfired due to unforeseen regulatory issues.
The politician's strategy to win over young voters misfired, alienating his core supporters instead.
